entries.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept of Physics (Meaning, branches and connection with other disciplines)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm different physical phenomena (natural and man-made) in their environment in relation to Physics
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through the use of ICT to come up with the meaning and branches of Physics
Field trip: Organise field trips to help students to explore the applications of Physics in real life experience
entries.assessment: Concept of Physics is clearly explained
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
entries.learning_activity: (b) Discuss the contribution of Physics to the development of modern society
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the contributions of Physics in the development of modern society
ICT-based learning: Guide the students through different Online sources and web-based information to explore contributions of Physics in the development of modern society
entries.assessment: Contributions of Physics to the development of modern society are clearly discussed
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
entries.learning_activity: (c) Explain concepts of physical quantities (fundamental and derived quantities) and their, SI units
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on measurable objects found in their environment
Jigsaw: Guide students through Jigsaw method to measure the size of various objects assign the SI Unit of the measured quantities
entries.assessment: The concepts of physical quantities are clearly explained
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
entries.learning_activity: (d) Describe concepts of linear motion (speed, velocity, acceleration, distance, and displacement)
teaching_learning_activities: Circle the sage: Guide students through circle-the-sage to explore the concept of linear motion
Questions and answers: Facilitate the students through questions and answers on the real-life applications of linear motion
ICT based learning: Guide students through the use of interactive simulations to visualize the concept of linear motion
entries.assessment: The concepts of linear motion are clearly described
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
entries.learning_activity: (e) Explain the concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure, work, power, energy
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure work, power and energy
ICT based learning: Guide students through the use of interactive simulations to visualize the concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure, work, power, energy
Questions and answers: Use questions and answers to guide students explaining the principle related to force, density, pressure, power and energy
entries.assessment: The concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure, work, power and energy are explained clearly
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
entries.learning_activity: (f) Deduce the relationship between density, sinking and floating
teaching_learning_activities: Experimentation: Guide the students to perform an experiment to deduce the relationship between density, sinking and floating
ICT-based learning: Guide student through the use of different online sources and web-based information to explore principles related to density, sinking and floating
entries.assessment: The relationship between density, sinking and floating is correctly deduced
resources: picture of floating and sinking objects and online resources
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Apply knowledge of Physics in various contexts
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic concepts, theories and principles of Physics
entries.learning_activity: (g) Describe the mechanical properties of matter in relation to force and energy
teaching_learning_activities: Think-ink pair share: Guide the students through Think ink pair share to explore the mechanical properties of matter in relation to force and energy
Group discussion: Organize students in manageable groups to discuss different terms related to mechanical properties of matter
Experimentation: Guide the students through experiment to investigate the relationship between various mechanical properties of matter
entries.assessment: The concepts and principles related to force, density, pressure, power and energy are explained clearly
resources: glass jar with cork and rubber band
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.2 Demonstrate mastery of basic terminologies, measurements and symbols in Physics
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe various instruments used for measurement in Physics
teaching_learning_activities: Circle-the-sage: Guide students through circle-the-sage strategy to describe various instruments used for measurements in Physics
Project work: Facilitate the students to describe various instruments used in Physics measurement
Virtual lab experiment: Facilitate the students through the use of Virtual lab to perform experiments to measure various objects
entries.assessment: Measuring instruments used in Physics measurements are correctly described
resources: vernier calliper and steel balls of varying diameters

entries.specific_competence: 1.3 Use mathematics to explain physical principles and phenomena

Problem solving: Guide the students to use physical principles to solve real life problems

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Conduct experiments in Physics
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic experimental skills in Physics
entries.learning_activity: (a) Conduct experiments related to linear motion, density, force, pressure, work, energy, power and mechanical properties of matter
teaching_learning_activities: Experimentation: Guide students through experiments to investigate the relationship between various parameters related to linear motion, density, force, pressure, work, energy, power and mechanical properties of matter

ICT based learning: Facilitate the students through the use of virtual lab, to investigate the principles related to linear motion, density, force, pressure, work, energy, power and mechanical properties of matter
entries.assessment: Experiments are properly conducted following the scientific method
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Conduct experiments in Physics
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Demonstrate mastery of data analysis, presentation and report writing in Physics
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explain the methods of analysing experimental data in physics (statistical analysis)
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Guide students in groups to investigate various methods used to analyse experimental data in physics (statistical analysis)
ICT based approach: Guide students through the use of ICT to investigate the best methods for analysing experimental data in Physics (statistical analysis)
entries.assessment: The methods of analysing experimental data are explained correctly
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Evaluate and use information in Physics
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Collect, describe and relate physical data
entries.learning_activity: (a) Collect and analyse data to explain various physical quantities (density, force, pressure)
teaching_learning_activities: Cooperative learning strategy: Guide students to share their understanding on how to collect and analyse data to explain various physical parameters (density, force, pressure)
Project work: Facilitate students to do projects to collect, analyse data and explain various physical parameters (density, force, pressure)
entries.assessment: The data used to explain various physical parameters are collected and analysed appropriately
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Evaluate and use information in Physics
entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Collect, describe and relate physical data
entries.learning_activity: (b) Collect and analyse data to explain experimental observations related to linear motion, force, density, pressure and mechanical properties of matter
teaching_learning_activities: ICT based approach: Guide students in utilizing interactive digital simulations to collect analyse data and explain experimental observations related to linear motion, force, density, pressure and mechanical properties of matter
Experimentation: Guide students in manageable groups to conduct experiments and analyse collected data
entries.assessment: The physical data to explain experimental observations related to given concepts are appropriately collected and analysed
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 3.0 Evaluate and use information in Physics
entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Carry out a project in Physics
entries.learning_activity: (a) Develop prototype devices based on the concepts, theories, principles and laws gained from the field of linear motion, force, density, pressure and mechanical properties of matter
teaching_learning_activities: Group discussion: Organize students in manageable groups to discuss the procedure for the development of prototype devices based on the concepts, theories, principles and laws gained from the field of linear motion, force, density, pressure and mechanical properties of matter
Problem based approach: Guide students to relate the designed prototypes and real life.
entries.assessment: Prototypes are developed based on Physics
entries.references: TIE, Physics for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
